data.title: Musk: Grok Imagine has achieved positive gross profit; video technology is key to AGI.
data.description: Elon Musk stated on the X platform that the core of AI development lies in video understanding and generation, claiming that "photons are the highest bandwidth form of communication," and that related technologies will become an important tool on the path to general artificial intelligence. He also pointed out that xAI's image generation tool, Grok Imagine, has achieved positive gross profit and is not a loss-making project. This statement comes against the backdrop of OpenAI's recent shutdown of its video platform, Sora. Public reports indicate that Sora generated approximately $2.1 million in revenue since its launch, with an average daily operating loss of approximately $1 million. Musk did not disclose specific revenue figures for Grok Imagine, but previously stated that he would continue to increase investment, believing it to have significant strategic value.

data.title: AI Developer Uncovers Cache Bugs in Claude Code
data.description:
A developer has identified two cache bugs in the standalone installation of Claude Code, according to PANews. The discovery was made through reverse engineering using Ghidra, MITM proxy, and radare2 on the 228MB binary file. These bugs could potentially increase API costs by 10-20 times without user awareness. The analysis has been submitted to GitHub (issue #40524) and has been marked by Anthropic as a regression bug for resolution.

The first bug is found in the custom Bun runtime used by the standalone version, where an error in the billing identifier replacement logic causes a complete cache rebuild with each request. The second bug affects users who utilize the --resume or --continue options to restore sessions. Introduced in version 2.1.69, this bug results in a cache prefix mismatch, leading to the entire conversation history being rewritten instead of being read from the cache. Anthropic engineers have previously confirmed that users are reaching usage limits much faster than anticipated.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Musk Highlights Video Understanding as Key to AI Development
data.description:
Elon Musk recently emphasized the importance of video understanding and generation in AI development on the X platform. According to Odaily, Musk described photons as the highest bandwidth communication form, suggesting that related technologies will be crucial for achieving general artificial intelligence. He also mentioned that xAI's image generation tool, Grok Imagine, has reached positive profitability and is not operating at a loss.

This statement comes in the context of OpenAI's recent shutdown of its video platform, Sora. Public reports indicate that Sora generated approximately $2.1 million in revenue since its launch, with an average daily operational loss of about $1 million. While Musk did not disclose specific revenue figures for Grok Imagine, he previously expressed plans to increase investment in the tool, citing its significant strategic value.

data.title: Gauntlet Takes Action Following Resolv Attack, Removes $11.9 Million in Liquidity
data.description:
Gauntlet, a risk management protocol, has announced measures following an attack on Resolv, which has yet to release a remediation plan. According to Foresight News, Gauntlet has removed over $11.9 million in liquidity from several Morpho vaults and plans to establish a claims contract for affected suppliers.

The specific actions taken include the removal of the wstUSR/USDC market from the USDC Core (v1) vault, involving $7.6 million in liquidity. Additionally, the USDC Frontier (v1.1) vault has been deprecated, and three related markets have been removed, affecting $4.3 million in liquidity. Several markets within the Resolv USDC (v1.1) vault will be removed after a three-day time lock, and the Seamless USDC and Extrafi XLend USDC related markets have also been completed.

data.title: Pakistani Foreign Minister to Visit China Amid Ongoing Iran Conflict
data.description: Pakistani Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar is preparing for a visit to China as the conflict in Iran enters its fifth week. Bloomberg posted on X that the ongoing war shows no signs of resolution, raising concerns about regional stability. The visit aims to strengthen diplomatic ties between Pakistan and China, focusing on regional security and economic cooperation. The situation in Iran remains tense, with international observers closely monitoring developments. Dar's visit underscores the importance of diplomatic engagement in addressing regional conflicts and fostering cooperation among neighboring countries.

data.title: Vitalik Buterin Acquires ZCHF Tokens in Recent Transaction
data.description: Vitalik Buterin has recently made a significant purchase of ZCHF tokens. According to Foresight News, Buterin spent 197,944 USDC to acquire 157,869 ZCHF tokens at an average price of 1.25 USD per token within the past six hours.

data.title: Billions Delays Token Generation Event Indefinitely
data.description:
The digital identity verification platform Billions has announced a delay in its token generation event (TGE), originally scheduled for completion by the second quarter. According to Foresight News, CEO Evin stated that there is currently no new specific launch date.

The postponement is attributed to three main factors: the market environment in the first quarter, the incomplete independent regulatory approval process with exchange partners, and the community's demand for a secure launch. The company has confirmed that the platform's infrastructure is fully developed and will inform the community as soon as a final date is agreed upon with exchange partners.

data.title: Bitget to launch EDGE spot trading
data.description: Bitget will be listing edgeX (EDGE) in the Innovation Zone. Deposits are now open, and trading will open on March 31 at 20:30 (UTC+8).

data.title: Nikkei Index Experiences Early Decline, Slight Recovery by Midday
data.description: The Nikkei Index saw a significant drop in early trading, falling nearly 1,330 points to reach a new low for the year at 50,558 points. According to RTHK, the index managed to recover some losses as it approached midday, experiencing a slight rebound. By the half-day mark, the index stood at 51,820 points, reflecting a decrease of 65 points or 0.13%. Edited by Song Jialiang.

data.title: Philippines Faces Inflation Surge Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: The ongoing conflict in Iran has led to a significant increase in domestic fuel prices, which is likely to have driven inflation in the import-dependent Philippines to its highest level in nearly two years. Bloomberg posted on X, citing the central bank's forecast, that the situation in Iran is impacting global oil markets, thereby affecting countries reliant on imports like the Philippines. The central bank's analysis suggests that the inflationary pressures are primarily due to the rising costs of fuel, which have a cascading effect on various sectors of the economy. This development comes as the Philippines grapples with economic challenges, including supply chain disruptions and fluctuating commodity prices. The central bank is closely monitoring the situation and may consider policy adjustments to mitigate the impact on the economy. The inflation spike underscores the vulnerability of import-dependent nations to global geopolitical events, highlighting the need for strategic economic planning and diversification.

data.title: Nasdaq will remove the 10% free float threshold and introduce fast-track inclusion rules.
data.description: Nasdaq will remove the 10% minimum free float requirement and introduce a "fast track" mechanism starting May 1, 2026. The new rules will allow mega-IPOs to be included in the Nasdaq 100 index within approximately 15 trading days. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Nasdaq to Remove 10% Minimum Float Requirement by 2026
data.description: Nasdaq has announced plans to eliminate the 10% minimum float requirement starting May 1, 2026. According to PANews, this change will be accompanied by the introduction of a 'fast track' rule, allowing companies with large market capitalizations to be included in the Nasdaq 100 Index within approximately 15 trading days of their initial public offering (IPO). This move is expected to streamline the process for high-value IPOs seeking quick entry into the index.

data.title: Hong Kong-Listed Sinohope Tech Rebrands as Bitfire Group
data.description: Hong Kong-listed company Sinohope Tech Holdings Limited (stock code: 1611.HK) has officially announced its rebranding. According to PANews, starting from 9 a.m. on March 31, 2026, the company's stock abbreviation on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange will change from 'Sinohope Tech' to 'Bitfire Group.' The new official website, www.bitfire.com, has also been launched. The company stated that this rebranding marks its transition from a regional fintech enterprise to a global, comprehensive digital financial group.

data.title: Huma Finance Alerts Users About Arf's Compromised Account
data.description: Huma Finance has issued a warning on the X platform regarding a security breach involving its partner, Arf. According to PANews, the official X account of Arf, @arf_one, has been compromised. Users are advised not to interact with any posts from this account until it is restored.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | UBS Strategist Predicts End of Gold Bull Market Amid Iran Conflict
data.description:
On March 31, a strategist from UBS suggested that the prolonged Iran conflict and sustained high oil prices might signal the end of the gold bull market. According to BlockBeats, Joni Teves, a precious metals strategist at the Swiss investment bank, stated in an interview last Friday that investors might be witnessing the final stages of the gold bull market. "We believe the gold price cycle largely aligns with the Federal Reserve's policy cycle, so we expect the rally to gradually fade by the end of the year, with gold prices consolidating at lower levels in the coming years," she explained.

Teves noted that UBS's baseline expectation is that after a period of consolidation, gold prices will reach new highs later this year as allocation funds continue to enter the market. "However, it is important to point out that the ongoing Middle East conflict could lead to significant changes in macroeconomic outlook and policy expectations, which could, in turn, alter the long-term trajectory of gold prices."

data.title: Trader Opens $11 Million Short Position on Bitcoin
data.description: According to Foresight News, The Data Nerd has reported that approximately one hour ago, the trader known as pension_usdt_eth opened a $11 million short position on Bitcoin with a 3x leverage. This trader has successfully profited from 19 consecutive orders since February 18.

data.title: Whale Reenters Brent Oil Market with Leveraged Position
data.description: A significant market player has made a notable move in the Brent oil market. According to BlockBeats On-chain Detection, within the past hour, a whale with an address starting with 0xcc1 closed a previous Brent oil long position at a loss. Subsequently, the whale reentered the market with a leveraged long position, utilizing 2x leverage. The current position amounts to $5.3 million, with an average price of $159.6 per unit. At present, the position shows a slight unrealized profit of approximately $20,000.

data.title: Cathay Pacific CEO Addresses Impact of Middle East Conflict on Fuel Prices
data.description:
Cathay Pacific CEO Ronald Lam has highlighted the challenges posed by rising aviation fuel prices due to ongoing conflicts in the Middle East. According to RTHK, Lam emphasized that the group's immediate priority is to maintain flight capacity, with any reduction in flights being considered a last resort.

In an interview with Reuters, Lam noted a significant decrease in passenger traffic from the Middle East since the outbreak of the conflict involving the U.S., Israel, and Iran. Conversely, there has been an increase in demand for long-haul flights to North America, Europe, and Australia.

Lam expressed concern over the cost of aviation fuel, stating that if prices remain at double the pre-conflict levels for an extended period, it will be challenging to sustain passenger and cargo demand.

data.title: U.S. Special Forces Arrive in Middle East Amid Ongoing Conflict
data.description:
The conflict between the United States and Iran has persisted for over a month, with reports indicating that hundreds of U.S. special forces soldiers have arrived in the Middle East to provide more options for military action against Iran. According to RTHK, the Pentagon is preparing for several weeks of ground operations in Iran.

In a related development, Yemen's Houthi forces recently launched missiles into Israeli territory, marking their first attack since the conflict began. Reports suggest that Israel interprets this as an intention to join the war. There is concern that if the conflict spreads from the Persian Gulf to the Red Sea, it could impact international energy routes and further affect the global economy.

data.title: Malaysia Raises 2026 Economic Growth Forecast Amid Middle East Conflict
data.description: Malaysia has revised its economic growth forecast for 2026, anticipating robust domestic demand and investment to mitigate the effects of the intensifying conflict in the Middle East. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Malaysia's optimism in its economic outlook despite regional geopolitical tensions. The government expects that increased local consumption and investment will play a crucial role in sustaining economic momentum. This adjustment reflects Malaysia's strategic focus on strengthening its internal economic drivers to counter external uncertainties.
